title: The Blackbanner Reaving
rumor: [Realm] The road-marshal Orsa is asking after sword-hands: a charismatic outlaw named Vharo Blackbanner is binding the scattered raiding camps into one host, and the burnt waystation at Redgate is only his opening move.

areas.name: Redgate Waystation
areas.legend: A coaching inn and toll-post on the trade road, its stockade thrown down and its stables burnt — the first place Blackbanner's united raiders struck. Marshal Orsa keeps a grim watch over the ashes.

areas.name: The Ambush Wood
areas.legend: A dense stretch of roadside timber hung with the raiders' rope-and-hook snares, where the camps stage their attacks on the caravans. A muster-roll lies dropped among the trampled ferns.

areas.name: The Blackbanner Camp
areas.legend: A palisaded outlaw camp of a hundred cook-fires beneath a great black standard, where Vharo Blackbanner holds court over the confederated raiding bands and plans the sack of the settlements.

marshal_orsa.name: Marshal Orsa
marshal_orsa.description: A scarred, plain-spoken road-marshal who has policed the trade roads for twenty years and never seen the camps unite until now. She has no soldiers left to spare and every reason to hire a blade.
goals: Break the outlaw confederation before it marches

caravan_master_deel.name: Caravan-Master Deel
caravan_master_deel.description: A shaken caravan-master who lost his goods and half his guards to the Ambush Wood and lived only by playing dead. He can tell you how the raiders fight now — together, and to a plan.
goals: Recover what's left of my trade

lieutenant_kessa.name: Kessa Fenn
lieutenant_kessa.description: One of Blackbanner's own lieutenants, keeping her own watch at the wood's edge. She joined the raiders to feed a starving village and did not sign on to sack towns — a word in her ear might turn her, and with her the other camps' captains.
goals: Feed my village without becoming a monster
Find a way out from under Blackbanner's banner
Protect the camp-folk who trusted me

resolved: With Vharo Blackbanner answered for, the confederated raiding camps have scattered — the trade road is the marshal's again, and far fewer outlaws prowl it.
